summary: Closed unmerged PR from an AI-assisted decompilation pipeline. The final revision was narrowed to 8 objdiff-verified 100% matches across Crazy Hand, Ice Mountain, Pura, and Old Kuri after reviewers objected to a broader raw-pointer-heavy AI batch. Main durable value is the review guidance: keep AI submissions tightly scoped to byte-perfect matches, avoid automated Copilot review noise, prefer M2C_FIELD/proper structs/inlines over raw pointer math, and use m2c flags/union-field hints to improve decomp quality. The PR was later closed because most changes were covered by other PRs.
what_changed: Final slice changed 4 files, adding 96 lines and deleting 29, after an earlier broader AI-assisted revision was reduced to only 8 claimed 100% matches.
In ftCh_Init.c, ftCh_Damage2_Anim changed Fighter_ChangeMotionState from ftMh_MS_Damage to ftMh_MS_Damage2.

In gricemt.c, grIceMt_801F7F1C and grIceMt_801F81B4 were filled in as two-entry ground material loops that walk user_data-derived data, check offset 0xF8 for a non-null HSD_GObj pointer, and pass it to grMaterial_801C8CDC.

In gricemt.c, grIceMt_801F929C replaced placeholder calls with a small animation state machine using s16 fields in arg1, calling mpLib_80057BC0, mpJointListAdd, and grAnime_801C83D0 at configured frame thresholds.

In gricemt.c, fn_801F9338 and fn_801F9448 were implemented as collision callback variants: they inspect CollData byte offset 0x34, initialize an Ice Mountain state flag if unset, trigger grAnime_801C7A04/grAnime_801C7B24/grAnime_801C78FC sequences, then delegate to grIceMt_801FA7F0.

In grpura.c, grPura_80212290 was changed to set a callback at gobj offset 0x1C to fn_802130D0, use toon texture image grPu_803E7620, process the JObj, clear the toon texture, and start stage animation with the Ground map_id.
In itoldkuri.c, it_802D747C changed from an empty stub to an item state-change sequence calling it_8027CAD8, it_8027C0A8(gobj, 0.0f, 5.0f), it_802756E0, and it_802D848C(gobj, 0, 2).
The PR body reported verification with objdiff for all 8 functions, ninja producing a matching DOL SHA1, melee-issues introducing 0 new issues, and EditorConfig whitespace cleanup.
The PR was not merged; PsiLupan closed it because most changes had been covered by other PRs.
smart_moves: The author responded to maintainer feedback by removing non-100% and partial-match functions instead of trying to merge a broad AI-generated batch.
The revised scope was easy to review: 8 named functions, 4 files, and explicit objdiff/ninja/melee-issues verification.
The PR captured process improvements for future AI-assisted decompilation: use m2c, Ghidra, objdiff, and decomp-permuter, but gate submissions on byte-perfect matches and project style.
The Crazy Hand change is a good example of a tiny enum correction producing a claimed 100% match without adding raw decomp noise.
The author accepted that some final functions still used pointer-style access only for matching and documented that M2C_FIELD/proper struct access should be taught to the pipeline.
decomp_lessons: Byte-perfect matching is necessary but not sufficient for review quality; raw pointer math can regress readability enough that maintainers request rescoping.
For m2c output, reviewer correction says --valid-syntax is what generates M2C_FIELD, while --no-casts cleans surrounding casts; using both gives a better AI starting point.

Use --union-field GroundVars:icemt for ground stage files when appropriate; BR- also identified FighterVars and ItemVars as other major union areas, though exact per-field flags were not fully specified in the PR text.
jobj.h inline functions can often be recognized from HSD_ASSERT-style string plus line-number patterns; future decomp should prefer existing inlines over re-emitting expanded assertion logic.

GroundVars/Ice Mountain has known ambiguity near offsets around xF8/x100: grIceMt_801F87FC reportedly treats nearby data as an HSD_GObj*, while fn_801F9338 treats it as s16; BR- suggested grIceMt_GroundVars2 stops at xF4 and likely has unmapped s16 data afterward.

Generic automated code review can be misleading for decompilation because faithful reproduction of original code may look like memory safety or UB bugs; maintainers said automated reviews were not useful here.
If a final match requires raw offsets, mark it as a stopgap and leave struct/header cleanup as follow-up rather than normalizing opaque pointer arithmetic.
naming_conventions: Prefer semantic local names such as gp, jobj, fp, and i when the role is obvious.
Placeholder/temp names such as temp_r31 or temp_r30 are acceptable when the variable purpose is not obvious, but reviewers prefer readable names when easy.
Keep anonymous callbacks as fn_<address> when no better semantic name is available, as with fn_801F9338, fn_801F9448, and fn_802130D0.
Preserve existing module prefixes: ftCh_ for Crazy Hand, grIceMt_ for Ice Mountain stage code, grPura_ for Pura stage code, and itOldkuri_/it_802D... for Old Kuri item code.
Prefer project typedefs and accessors/proper fields such as HSD_GObj, Ground_GObj, Ground, Item_GObj, HSD_JObj, GET_GROUND, and M2C_FIELD where matching permits.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use objdiff to confirm each submitted function is 100% byte-perfect against NTSC 1.02 before submitting.
Use ninja/DOL SHA1 matching as whole-build verification, not just local function objdiff.
Use melee-issues to ensure no new project issues are introduced; this PR reported 0 new issues and 7 pre-existing unrelated issues.
PAD_STACK(8) and dummy stack arrays such as u8 _[8] appeared in the final diff to preserve stack layout for matches.

Manual byte-offset access was used where structs were incomplete or ambiguous, including offsets 0xF8, 0x100, CollData +0x34, and GObj +0x1C; reviewers considered this a workaround, not an ideal style.
Function signatures and enum choices can be part of matching, shown by grIceMt_801F7F1C changing to HSD_GObj* and ftCh_Damage2_Anim using ftMh_MS_Damage2.
For command/data pointer work in future AI output, be careful about C pointer arithmetic units; review comments on the earlier revision noted that u32* + 4 advances 16 bytes, not 4.
review_feedback: ribbanya requested changes: raw pointer math considerably regressed quality/readability, so the PR should contain only 100% matches and functions not relying on that strategy; M2C_FIELD could be a workable middle ground.
ribbanya requested recognizing jobj.h inline functions rather than using expanded m2c inline output.
ribbanya said automated code reviews are not useful, especially when one agent reviews another agent's decompilation.
BR- suggested m2c flags and union hints, especially --union-field GroundVars:icemt, to reduce strange GroundVars accesses.
BR- preferred variables named gp or jobj over temp_r31/temp_r30 when obvious, while later clarifying that placeholder names are fine if meaning is unclear.
BR- corrected that --valid-syntax generates M2C_FIELD and --no-casts removes surrounding casts; he uses both.
BR- noted FighterVars and ItemVars as other major union areas to consider for union-field handling.

BR- documented an Ice Mountain struct gap/typing concern around xF8: some code treats nearby data as HSD_GObj*, while fn_801F9338 treats it as s16, suggesting unmapped s16 data after grIceMt_GroundVars2 xF4.

Copilot review comments in the dump targeted an earlier 23-file revision and flagged issues such as fixed-size allocations, pointer increments, null dereferences, stack out-of-bounds writes, and varargs mismatches; human review treated automated review as not useful for this decomp PR.
PsiLupan closed the PR because most of the changes had already been covered by other PRs.
follow_up_queries: Find the later PRs that covered the grIceMt, grPura, ftCh_Damage2_Anim, and it_802D747C changes from PR2373.
Search for subsequent struct/header work that resolves GroundVars/Ice Mountain offsets near xF8, x100, and grIceMt_GroundVars2 xF4.
Search for decomp PRs using m2c --valid-syntax --no-casts and M2C_FIELD to see whether AI-assisted output quality improved.
Search for PRs touching jobj.h inline recognition or HSD_ASSERT string/line-number inline boundaries.
Search later PRs by zalo or decomp-research-ai for whether automated union-field selection by /gr/, /ft/, and /it/ paths was reused.
Check whether grPura_80212290 uses grPu_803E7620 and callback fn_802130D0 in merged master or was superseded by a cleaner struct-field version.
